openmeter/cost.purpose: Computes feature cost by querying ClickHouse meter usage via streaming.Connector and resolving per-unit costs from feature configuration (manual or LLM-based). Two-layer domain: adapter/ owns ClickHouse queries and cost resolution; service/ owns input validation and delegation.

patterns.name: Pre-resolve LLM prices once per query
patterns.description: Call getLLMPrices to build a price cache keyed on (provider, model) before iterating rows — never call llmcostService.ResolvePrice inside the per-row loop.
patterns.example: prices, err := a.getLLMPrices(ctx, namespace, feature); // then pass prices to resolveUnitCost

patterns.name: Clone GroupBy/FilterGroupBy before merging
patterns.description: Never mutate the caller's streaming.QueryParams.GroupBy or FilterGroupBy slices/maps; always slices.Clone and build a new merged map.
patterns.example: merged := maps.Clone(feature.MeterGroupByFilters); for k, v := range params.FilterGroupBy { merged[k] = v }

patterns.name: costResolverFunc abstraction
patterns.description: Cost resolution is injected as a function type (costResolverFunc), not an interface, separating LLM vs. manual resolution from aggregation logic.
patterns.example: var resolver costResolverFunc = func(tokenType string) (*ResolvedUnitCost, error) { ... }

patterns.name: pricing-not-found → detail string, not hard error
patterns.description: In costResolverFunc, convert models.IsGenericNotFoundError to a detail string so the row is still emitted rather than aborting the query.
patterns.example: if models.IsGenericNotFoundError(err) { return nil, nil /* populate row.Detail instead */ }

patterns.name: Service validates then delegates
patterns.description: Service.QueryFeatureCost calls input.Validate() then calls cost.Adapter.QueryFeatureCost — no business logic or data access in the service.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return s.adapter.QueryFeatureCost(ctx, input)

key_file_guides.role: Defines cost.Adapter interface with single method QueryFeatureCost.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Interface has only one method; keep adapter.go as the contract, implementation lives in adapter/ sub-package.

key_file_guides.role: Defines cost.Service interface plus all input/output types (QueryFeatureCostInput, CostQueryResult, CostQueryRow, ResolvedUnitCost).
key_file_guides.watch_for: QueryFeatureCostInput.Validate() uses models.NewNillableGenericValidationError — replicate this pattern for any new input types.

key_file_guides.role: Concrete adapter; wires streaming.Connector, feature resolver, llmcost service, and costResolverFunc together.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Internal group-by keys injected into query params must be stripped from output rows — omitting the strip leaks internal fields to callers.

key_file_guides.role: Per-row cost computation: resolveUnitCost switch + costPerTokenForType.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New cost resolution types require a new switch branch in resolveUnitCost AND a costPerTokenForType case if token-type based.

anti_patterns: Calling llmcostService.ResolvePrice inside the per-row cost loop — always pre-resolve via getLLMPrices first.
Passing *entdb.Client or touching Ent directly — this adapter is ClickHouse-only via streaming.Connector.
Mutating the caller's streaming.QueryParams.GroupBy or FilterGroupBy slices/maps in place — always clone before merging.
Returning a hard error for pricing-not-found in costResolverFunc — convert to a detail string so the row is still emitted.
Adding computation or data access directly in service methods — delegate entirely to cost.Adapter.

decisions.decision: Price cache keyed on (provider, model) not (provider, model, token_type).
decisions.rationale: Token-type breakdown happens inside resolveUnitCost after the cache lookup; caching at the coarser key reduces LLM-cost service calls.

decisions.decision: Internal group-by keys injected into query params and stripped from output.
decisions.rationale: ClickHouse queries require additional group-by dimensions for cost resolution that must not surface in the public CostQueryRow.GroupBy map.

decisions.decision: costResolverFunc is a function type rather than an interface.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the resolution strategy as a simple closure without requiring a named type, making it easy to swap LLM vs. manual resolution at construction time.

code_examples.scenario: Implementing a new cost adapter method that queries ClickHouse

openmeter/credit.purpose: Manages credit grants and balance snapshots for metered entitlements. CreditConnector (= BalanceConnector + GrantConnector) is the public facade; engine/ computes burn-down without I/O; adapter/ sub-packages persist via Ent in transaction-aware repos; grant burn-down requires granularity truncation to time.Minute on all effective times.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wraps all multi-step writes
patterns.description: CreateGrant and VoidGrant both use transaction.Run(ctx, m.GrantRepo, ...) and then call m.GrantRepo.WithTx(ctx, tx) inside to stay on the ctx-bound transaction.
patterns.example: transaction.Run(ctx, m.GrantRepo, func(ctx context.Context) (*grant.Grant, error) { tx, _ := entutils.GetDriverFromContext(ctx); return m.GrantRepo.WithTx(ctx, tx).CreateGrant(ctx, inp) })

patterns.name: LockOwnerForTx before any write
patterns.description: All mutating connector methods call m.OwnerConnector.LockOwnerForTx(ctx, ownerID, ...) before writing grants or snapshots to prevent concurrent balance races.
patterns.example: err = m.OwnerConnector.LockOwnerForTx(ctx, ownerID, true)

patterns.name: GetLastValidSnapshotAt falls back to start-of-measurement
patterns.description: When no snapshot exists (NoSavedBalanceForOwnerError), create a zero-balance snapshot from GetStartOfMeasurement + NewStartingMap(grants) — do not propagate the not-found error.
patterns.example: if _, ok := lo.ErrorsAs[*balance.NoSavedBalanceForOwnerError](err); ok { startOfMeasurement, _ = m.OwnerConnector.GetStartOfMeasurement(ctx, owner); bal = balance.Snapshot{At: startOfMeasurement, ...} }

patterns.name: buildEngineForOwner caches period boundaries
patterns.description: Build a period cache from SortedPeriodsFromDedupedTimes before constructing the engine, then resolve GetUsagePeriodStartAt from the cache inside the UsageQuerier closure — never call the owner connector per usage query.
patterns.example: periodCache := SortedPeriodsFromDedupedTimes(times); GetUsagePeriodStartAt: func(..., at time.Time) (time.Time, error) { for _, p := range periodCache { if p.ContainsInclusive(at) { return p.From, nil } } }

patterns.name: Granularity truncation on all times
patterns.description: All effective times and reset times are truncated to m.Granularity (time.Minute) before engine use and before storing grants or snapshots.
patterns.example: input.EffectiveAt = input.EffectiveAt.Truncate(granularity)

patterns.name: snapshotEngineResult acquires non-blocking lock and silently skips on failure
patterns.description: Snapshotting is an optimisation; failure to acquire the lock (non-blocking LockOwnerForTx with false) should not abort the balance query that triggered it.
patterns.example: if err := transaction.RunWithNoValue(ctx, m.GrantRepo, func(ctx context.Context) error { return m.OwnerConnector.LockOwnerForTx(ctx, snapParams.owner, false) }); err != nil { return nil }

key_file_guides.role: Defines CreditConnector interface (BalanceConnector + GrantConnector), CreditConnectorConfig, and the connector struct. NewCreditConnector is the sole constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreditConnectorConfig is embedded by value in connector — adding fields to config is automatically visible to all methods.

key_file_guides.role: Implements BalanceConnector: GetBalanceAt, GetBalanceForPeriod, ResetUsageForOwner, GetLastValidSnapshotAt.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetBalanceAt and GetBalanceForPeriod both truncate-up to the next minute (FIXME note in code) — any new method must replicate this truncation.

key_file_guides.role: Implements GrantConnector: CreateGrant, VoidGrant. Also defines GrantConnector interface and CreateGrantInput.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CreateGrantInput.Validate() uses ValidationIssue (ErrGrantAmountMustBePositive.WithAttr) — follow the same pattern for new input fields.

key_file_guides.role: Internal engine-building helpers: buildEngineForOwner, snapshotEngineResult, saveSnapshot, populateBalanceSnapshotWithMissingGrantsActiveAt, removeInactiveGrantsFromSnapshotAt, SortedPeriodsFromDedupedTimes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: removeInactiveGrantsFromSnapshotAt returns an error if a grant in the snapshot balances map is not in the grants slice — callers must pass the full grants slice used for engine.Run.

key_file_guides.role: Package-level ValidationIssue error vars with HTTP status attributes.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New validation errors should follow models.NewValidationIssue + commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute pattern.

key_file_guides.role: ctrace singleton providing typed OTel span start options (WithOwner, WithPeriod, WithEngineParams).
key_file_guides.watch_for: All new connector methods should add a span via m.Tracer.Start and use cTrace helpers for structured attributes.

anti_patterns: Calling GrantRepo or BalanceSnapshotService methods without wrapping in transaction.Run when inside a multi-step write — bypasses ctx-bound transaction.
Omitting LockOwnerForTx before any grant/snapshot mutation — causes balance races under concurrent requests.
Propagating balance.NoSavedBalanceForOwnerError to callers — always fall back to start-of-measurement snapshot.
Querying GetUsagePeriodStartAt inside the QueryUsageFn callback at runtime — build the period cache in buildEngineForOwner instead.
Passing the caller's grants slice to the engine without populating missing grants via populateBalanceSnapshotWithMissingGrantsActiveAt — engine will error on unknown grant IDs in the snapshot.

decisions.decision: Period cache built once in buildEngineForOwner, resolved in-memory by UsageQuerier closure.
decisions.rationale: QueryUsageFn is called many times during engine.Run; caching period start times avoids O(n) DB calls per burn phase.

decisions.decision: snapshotEngineResult acquires a non-blocking lock and silently skips on failure.
decisions.rationale: Snapshotting is an optimisation; failure to acquire the lock should not abort the balance query that triggered it.

decisions.decision: All times truncated to Granularity (time.Minute) before engine use.
decisions.rationale: ClickHouse stores events in minute-window chunks; sub-minute precision causes incorrect burn-down calculations.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new multi-step write method to the credit connector

openmeter/currencies.purpose: Manages custom currencies and fiat-exchange cost bases for billing namespaces. Two-layer domain: adapter/ owns Ent/PostgreSQL persistence with full TransactingRepo discipline; service/ merges in-memory GOBL fiat enumeration with DB-stored custom currencies.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapping every DB method
patterns.description: Every adapter method body is wrapped with entutils.TransactingRepo or TransactingRepoWithNoValue to honor the ctx-bound transaction.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) (Currency, error) { row, err := tx.db.Currency.Create()...; return mapCurrencyFromDB(row), err })

patterns.name: Tx / WithTx / Self triad on adapter
patterns.description: adapter implements entutils.TxCreator (Tx method) and TxUser[*adapter] (WithTx + Self) so callers can join or start transactions uniformly.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { return &adapter{db: entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()).Client()} }

patterns.name: Input Validate() before transaction.Run
patterns.description: Service calls params.Validate() before calling transaction.Run; constraint errors from the DB are a last resort, not the primary validation gate.
patterns.example: if err := params.Validate(); err != nil { return Currency{}, err }; return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (Currency, error) { return s.adapter.CreateCurrency(ctx, params) })

patterns.name: entdb.IsConstraintError → GenericConflictError
patterns.description: Adapter maps Ent constraint violations to models.NewGenericConflictError, not raw Ent errors.
patterns.example: if entdb.IsConstraintError(err) { return Currency{}, models.NewGenericConflictError(err) }

patterns.name: mapXFromDB mapper functions
patterns.description: Separate top-level mapper functions (mapCurrencyFromDB, mapCostBasisFromDB) convert Ent rows to domain types — not methods on Ent types.
patterns.example: func mapCurrencyFromDB(row *entdb.Currency) Currency { return Currency{Code: row.Code, ...} }

patterns.name: In-memory fiat enumeration via GOBL
patterns.description: Service lists fiat currencies from GOBL at runtime (not from DB); in-memory merge and pagination of fiat + custom sets belongs in the service, not the adapter.
patterns.example: fiatCurrencies := gobl.AllCurrencies(); result := mergeAndPaginate(fiatCurrencies, customCurrencies, params.Page)

key_file_guides.role: Defines Adapter interface (CurrenciesAdapter + entutils.TxCreator) and CurrenciesAdapter sub-interface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adapter embeds TxCreator — any new implementation must provide Tx/WithTx/Self.

key_file_guides.role: All domain types (Currency, CostBasis) and all input types with Validate() implementations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All input structs implement models.Validator via var _ models.Validator = (*XInput)(nil) — add the assertion for any new input type.

key_file_guides.role: Defines CurrencyService interface — the public facade for HTTP handlers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Service interface must stay in sync with the adapter methods it delegates to; fiat-vs-custom logic lives here not in the adapter.

key_file_guides.role: Concrete Ent adapter; Config struct, Validate(), New() constructor, Tx/WithTx/Self.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Config.Validate() checks adapter dependencies are non-nil — replicate for any new dependency added to Config.

key_file_guides.role: Per-method DB implementations for ListCustomCurrencies, CreateCurrency, CreateCostBasis, ListCostBases.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every method must wrap with entutils.TransactingRepo — omitting it bypasses the caller's transaction.

anti_patterns: Calling a.db.X directly inside a method body without entutils.TransactingRepo — bypasses ctx-bound transactions.
Importing openmeter/ent/db in the service layer — all DB access must go through the currencies.Adapter interface.
Storing fiat currencies in the DB — they are enumerated in-memory from GOBL and merged at query time in the service.
Returning raw Ent errors to callers — always map constraint errors to models.GenericConflictError and not-found to models.GenericNotFoundError.
Constructing *adapter directly instead of calling adapter.New(Config{...}) — Validate() in New() enforces required deps.

decisions.decision: Fiat currency list sourced in-memory from GOBL, not stored in DB.
decisions.rationale: Fiat currencies are a stable enumeration; storing them would require migrations on standard updates and create drift risk.

decisions.decision: Tx/WithTx/Self triad on the adapter struct, not a shared TxWrapper type.
decisions.rationale: Currencies is a small domain; a single adapter file is simpler and mirrors the pattern used across other small domain adapters.

decisions.decision: EffectiveFrom defaulting and future-date validation in service, not adapter.
decisions.rationale: Business rules around time-validity belong in the service layer; the adapter only persists what it is given.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new adapter method for a new currencies entity

openmeter/debug.purpose: Exposes a single ClickHouse-backed debug metrics endpoint returning per-subject event counts in OpenMetrics (Prometheus) format. Minimal two-file domain: debug.go owns the DebugConnector interface and implementation wrapping streaming.Connector; httpdriver/ translates HTTP to domain calls.

patterns.name: DebugConnector as the only service interface
patterns.description: Business logic lives in debugConnector wrapping streaming.Connector; the httpdriver delegates to DebugConnector, never to streaming.Connector directly.
patterns.example: connector := debug.NewDebugConnector(streamingConnector); handler := httpdriver.NewHandler(connector, ...)

patterns.name: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs for every HTTP endpoint
patterns.description: Handlers use httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs, not http.Handler directly; namespace is resolved via namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder injected into the handler.
patterns.example: httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs(func(ctx context.Context, r *http.Request) (string, error) { return namespaceDecoder(ctx) }, operationFn, encodeResponse)

patterns.name: Per-handler error encoder appended via httptransport.AppendOptions
patterns.description: Error encoders are appended to shared options with httptransport.AppendOptions rather than replacing h.options.
patterns.example: opts := httptransport.AppendOptions(h.options, httptransport.WithErrorEncoder(myEncoder))

key_file_guides.role: Defines DebugConnector interface and debugConnector implementation that queries streaming.CountEvents and formats Prometheus OpenMetrics output.
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetDebugMetrics queries from start-of-day UTC — hardcoded time window; any new metrics methods should document their query window clearly.

key_file_guides.role: HTTP handler for the GetMetrics endpoint using httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Handler returns plain text (OpenMetrics), not JSON; do not add JSON encoder without also updating the Content-Type handling.

anti_patterns: Implementing http.Handler directly instead of using httptransport.NewHandlerWithArgs.
Reading namespace from URL path or query params instead of namespacedriver.NamespaceDecoder.
Replacing h.options instead of appending with httptransport.AppendOptions when adding error encoders.
Putting metrics computation inside the decode or encode functions — delegate to DebugConnector.
Adding streaming.Connector as a direct dependency in the httpdriver — always go through DebugConnector.

decisions.decision: DebugConnector wraps streaming.Connector rather than exposing streaming directly.
decisions.rationale: Keeps the httpdriver decoupled from ClickHouse query details; DebugConnector owns the Prometheus format conversion.

openmeter/dedupe.purpose: Provides CloudEvent deduplication for the sink worker via the Deduplicator interface. Two implementations: memorydedupe/ (LRU, no-dependency fallback) and redisdedupe/ (TTL-based, pipelined batch, three key-format modes for zero-downtime migration). All callers depend on dedupe.Deduplicator; implementations are swapped by config.

patterns.name: Deduplicator interface as the contract boundary
patterns.description: All callers depend on dedupe.Deduplicator; implementations are swapped by config without callers knowing which backend is active.
patterns.example: var d dedupe.Deduplicator = memorydedupe.New(size) // or redisdedupe.New(cfg)

patterns.name: Item.Key() as the canonical dedup key
patterns.description: All implementations key their stores on item.Key() = "namespace-source-id"; do not use raw event IDs or namespaces alone.
patterns.example: key := item.Key() // "myns-mysource-event123"

patterns.name: SET NX + TTL for Redis atomic set-if-not-exists
patterns.description: redisdedupe uses SetArgs{NX: true, TTL: d.ttl} to atomically write-if-absent; never use plain SET which overwrites existing keys.
patterns.example: d.Redis.SetArgs(ctx, key, val, redis.SetArgs{NX: true, TTL: d.ttl})

patterns.name: Pipelined batch writes in redisdedupe
patterns.description: Set() batches multiple keys into a single Redis pipeline rather than individual calls.
patterns.example: pipe := d.Redis.Pipeline(); for _, item := range items { pipe.SetArgs(ctx, item.Key(), ...) }; pipe.Exec(ctx)

patterns.name: Three-mode key format with explicit migration mode
patterns.description: redisdedupe supports rawkey, keyhash, and keyhash-migration modes; every key-operation method must switch on d.mode.
patterns.example: switch d.mode { case ModeRawKey: return item.Key(); case ModeKeyHash: return GetKeyHash(item); ... }

patterns.name: ContainsOrAdd for atomic check-and-set in memorydedupe
patterns.description: IsUnique uses LRU.ContainsOrAdd rather than separate Contains + Add calls to avoid TOCTOU races under concurrent ingest.
patterns.example: existed, _ := lru.ContainsOrAdd(item.Key(), nil)

key_file_guides.role: Defines Deduplicator interface, Item, Item.Key(), CheckUniqueBatchResult.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IsUnique is marked TODO/deprecated in favor of CheckUnique + Set; prefer the newer methods in new code.

key_file_guides.role: LRU-backed Deduplicator; IsUnique uses ContainsOrAdd for atomic check-and-set.
key_file_guides.watch_for: LRU stores nil values keyed by item.Key() — do not store event payload data.

key_file_guides.role: Redis-backed Deduplicator with pipeline batch and NX TTL semantics.
key_file_guides.watch_for: redis.Nil in pipeline results signals a pre-existing key (already deduped), not an error — handle explicitly.

key_file_guides.role: GetKeyHash uses xxh3-128 + base64url encoding.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Changing the hash algorithm requires a new migration mode and key rotation plan — never change silently.

anti_patterns: Using Redis SET without NX — overwrites existing keys and defeats deduplication.
Adding a new DedupeMode without updating every method's switch statement in redisdedupe.
Ignoring redis.Nil in pipeline results — it signals a pre-existing key, not an error.
Storing event payload data in the LRU cache — only nil values, keyed by item.Key().
Skipping Close() implementation on a new Deduplicator — must exist even as a no-op to satisfy the interface.

decisions.decision: LRU eviction in memorydedupe over unbounded map growth.
decisions.rationale: Sink worker processes high-volume events; unbounded growth would exhaust memory on long-running sinks.

decisions.decision: Three-mode key format (rawkey, keyhash, keyhash-migration) in redisdedupe.
decisions.rationale: Changing the key format without a migration mode would leave pre-existing keys unreachable and break deduplication during rollout.

decisions.decision: xxh3-128 + base64url over SHA-224 or raw composite strings.
decisions.rationale: xxh3 is significantly faster for high-throughput batch hashing; base64url keeps keys URL-safe and compact.
